revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:日本歴史地名大系]][[zh:日本歷史地名大系]]
Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei (日本歴史地名大系, "Comprehensive History of Japanese Place Names") is a geographical dictionary published by {{ill|Heibonsha|en|Heibonsha|qid=Q11482108|lt=Heibonsha|1=Heibonsha}}. It consists of 50 volumes: 48 volumes covering the 47 [[:en:prefectures|prefectures]] and [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]], and 2 index volumes.
The dictionary follows in the tradition of [[Yoshida Tōgo]]'s ''{{ill|Dai Nihon Chimei Jisho|ja|大日本地名辞書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Nihon Chimei Jisho|1=Dai Nippon Chimei Jisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17213149}}'' and is a contemporary of ''{{ill|Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4093011}}''.
== History == Around {{ill|1974|en|1974|qid=Q2478|lt=1974|1=1974}} (Showa 49), the project began with a proposal by [[Tanigawa Kenichi]] to compile a place name dictionary that would surpass the pre-war ''Dai Nihon Chimei Jisho''<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.japanknowledge.com/contents/serial/heritage/rekishi/1.html |title=Nippon Shomotsu Isan: Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei - Episode 1 |publisher=[[JapanKnowledge]] |accessdate=2014-03-12 |archivedate=2014-03-13 |url-status=dead}}</ref>. Editorial committees were organized for each prefecture, primarily involving local university historians. These committees collaborated with amateur historians and local experts to divide the writing tasks.
Starting with the publication of ''Nagano-ken no Chimei'' in {{ill|1979|en|1979|qid=Q2484|lt=1979|1=1979}} (Showa 54), the series took over 20 years to complete. The final volume, ''Fukuoka-ken no Chimei,'' was published in {{ill|2004|en|2004|qid=Q2014|lt=2004|1=2004}} (Heisei 16), the same year the series received the {{ill|Kikuchi Kan Prize|en|Kikuchi Kan Prize|qid=Q1000104|lt=Kikuchi Kan Prize|1=Kikuchi Kan Prize}}<ref>[http://www.bunshun.co.jp/award/kikuchi/list4.htm Kikuchi Prize Recipients List]</ref>. The series was completed in {{ill|2005|en|2005|qid=Q2019|lt=2005|1=2005}} (Heisei 17) with the publication of two supplementary index volumes.
Around the same time, ''Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten'' by {{ill|Kadokawa Shoten|en|Kadokawa Shoten|qid=Q1054765|lt=Kadokawa Shoten|1=Kadokawa Shoten}} was being compiled, but its Completion tier 2]] (Heisei 9), temple and shrine entries were extracted and expanded into three separate dictionaries: ''Kyoto/Yamashiro Jiin Jinja Daijiten,'' ''Yamato/Kii Jiin Jinja Daijiten,'' and ''Ōmi/Wakasa/Echizen Jiin Jinja Daijiten''<ref name=t>{{Cite web|url=http://rekishi.jkn21.com/contents/intro/rekishi/feature01.html |title=Features of the JapanKnowledge Edition |archivedate=2014-03-13 |url-status=dead}}</ref>.
On October 1, {{ill|2006|en|2006|qid=Q2021|lt=2006|1=2006}} (Heisei 18), the series was digitized and made available online through [[JapanKnowledge]]. Updates included incorporating the latest [[:en:Archaeological|Archaeological]] findings and adjusting for municipal mergers of the time<ref name=t></ref>. However, while search functionality continues to adapt to ongoing mergers, the text itself remains unchanged.
== Structure == * General Overview * {{ill|Provinces of Japan|en|Provinces of Japan|qid=Q860290|lt=Old Province Names|1=Provinces of Japan}} Section * Regional Section - Divided into major entries by municipalities at the time of publication. * Bibliographic Notes * {{ill|Administrative divisions of Japan|en|Administrative divisions of Japan|qid=Q1850442|lt=Administrative District|1=Administrative divisions of Japan}} Changes and [[Rice Tax|Stone Yields]] * [[:en:gojuon|gojuon]] Index * List of [[Difficult Kanji in Place Names|Hard-to-Read Place Names]]
== Volumes == {| class="sortable wikitable" style="line-height:1.4em; font-size:95%;" |-style="white-space:nowrap;" ! Volume !! Content !! Supervising/Editorial Committee !! Published !! ISBN |- |01||Hokkaido no Chimei||Hideo Nagai (Hokkaido University)||October 20, 2003||ISBN 4582490018 |- |02||Aomori-ken no Chimei||Toshiyoshi Torao (Hirosaki University)||July 10, 1982||ISBN 4582490026 |- |03||Iwate-ken no Chimei||Yoshiharu Mori (Iwate University), Seiichi Konno (Morioka Junior College)||July 13, 1990||ISBN 4582490034 |- |...||...||...||...||... |- |50||Classification Index||||January
2005||ISBN 4582490506 |}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Dai Nihon Chimei Jisho|ja|大日本地名辞書|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Nihon Chimei Jisho|1=Dai Nippon Chimei Jisho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q17213149}} * {{ill|Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|ja|角川日本地名大辞典|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q4093011}}
== External Links == * [https://japanknowledge.com/contents/rekishi/index.html JapanKnowledge Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei]
{{translated page|ja|日本歴史地名大系|version=101097568|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--Japanese Historical Topography Series--><!--Japanese Historical Topography Series--><!--Japanese Historical Topography Series--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:日本歷史地名大系]] {{wikidata link|Q17213509}} [[Category:Heibonsha]] [[Category:Japanese Gazetteers]] [[Category:Japanese Historical Series]] [[Category:Post-war Japanese historical texts]] [[Category:Publications disestablished in 2005]] [[Category:Publications established in 1979]] [[Category:Recipients of the Kikuchi Kan Prize]] [[Category:trailed the ''Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei'' by about 15 years.
In {{ill|1997|en|1997|qid=Q2088|lt=1997|1=1997}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
